message:
rulake@2.2.0 published to crates.io; restructure rulake-wasm for npm

- crates.io: published `rulake v2.2.0` from this machine using gcloud's
  CRATES_API_KEY. `cargo add rulake` now works.

- rulake-wasm restructure:
  Initial publish at 2.2.0 only shipped README + package.json — npm
  treats subdirs containing their own package.json as nested packages
  and excludes them from the parent tarball. wasm-pack's three pkg-*
  outputs (web / nodejs / bundler) each have their own package.json,
  so all the actual .wasm + .js files were dropped on the floor.

  Fix: flatten the `bundler` target's files to package root (works for
  Vite, webpack, Rollup, esbuild, Next, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun),
  keep `web/` and `nodejs/` as variant subdirs WITHOUT inner
  package.json files (rm'd post-build). New tarball: 7 files, 75 KB —
  including the 153 KB rulake_wasm_bg.wasm. npm pack --dry-run confirms.

  Bump to 2.2.1 because npm refuses to republish a name within 24h of
  unpublish. The version 2.2.0 slot is permanently retired regardless;
  `npm install rulake-wasm` will work once 24h elapses and 2.2.1 ships.

- node/package.json optionalDependencies: bumped `rulake-wasm` pin from
  2.2.0 → 2.2.1 to match what's republishable. Optional dep, so until
  republished npm just silently skips it (no install error).

Next: push tag node-v2.2.0 → release-node.yml builds 5 native binaries
in CI (linux-x64-gnu, linux-arm64-gnu, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64,
win32-x64-msvc) and publishes per-platform packages + umbrella `rulake`
to npm using the mirrored NPM_TOKEN.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -1,40 +1,39 @@
 {
   "name": "rulake-wasm",
-  "version": "2.2.0",
+  "version": "2.2.1",
   "description": "Memory Lake for Agentic AI — WASM build for browsers, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun. Same witness-anchored memory model as `rulake` (Node.js bindings), feature-reduced surface for edge runtimes (no AVX-512, no rayon).",
   "type": "module",
-  "main": "pkg-nodejs/rulake_wasm.js",
-  "module": "pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js",
-  "browser": "pkg-web/rulake_wasm.js",
-  "types": "pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
+  "main": "rulake_wasm.js",
+  "module": "rulake_wasm.js",
+  "types": "rulake_wasm.d.ts",
   "exports": {
     ".": {
-      "types": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
-      "browser": "./pkg-web/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "deno": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "worker": "./pkg-web/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "bun": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "import": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "require": "./pkg-nodejs/rulake_wasm.js",
-      "default": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js"
+      "types": "./rulake_wasm.d.ts",
+      "browser": "./web/rulake_wasm.js",
+      "worker": "./web/rulake_wasm.js",
+      "deno": "./rulake_wasm.js",
+      "bun": "./rulake_wasm.js",
+      "import": "./rulake_wasm.js",
+      "require": "./nodejs/rulake_wasm.js",
+      "default": "./rulake_wasm.js"
     },
     "./web": {
-      "types": "./pkg-web/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
-      "default": "./pkg-web/rulake_wasm.js"
+      "types": "./web/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
+      "default": "./web/rulake_wasm.js"
     },
     "./nodejs": {
-      "types": "./pkg-nodejs/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
-      "default": "./pkg-nodejs/rulake_wasm.js"
-    },
-    "./bundler": {
-      "types": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
-      "default": "./pkg-bundler/rulake_wasm.js"
+      "types": "./nodejs/rulake_wasm.d.ts",
+      "default": "./nodejs/rulake_wasm.js"
     }
   },
   "files": [
-    "pkg-web/",
-    "pkg-nodejs/",
-    "pkg-bundler/",
+    "rulake_wasm.js",
+    "rulake_wasm.d.ts",
+    "rulake_wasm_bg.js",
+    "rulake_wasm_bg.wasm",
+    "rulake_wasm_bg.wasm.d.ts",
+    "web/",
+    "nodejs/",
     "README.md"
   ],
   "keywords": [

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+/* tslint:disable */
+/* eslint-disable */
+
+/**
+ * Build version string. Useful for browser-console diagnostics.
+ */
+export function buildInfo(): string;
+
+/**
+ * Compute the SHAKE-256(32) witness for the load-bearing bundle fields.
+ *
+ * Useful for "I have these fields, what witness should the bundle
+ * carry?" — building bundles client-side, validating publishers, etc.
+ * Generation accepts either a number (encoded as `Num`) or a string
+ * (encoded as `Opaque`).
+ */
+export function computeWitness(data_ref: string, dim: number, rotation_seed: bigint, rerank_factor: number, generation: any): string;
+
+/**
+ * `format_version` of bundles this build understands.
+ */
+export function formatVersion(): number;
+
+/**
+ * Exact top-K nearest-neighbor search by squared L2 distance.
+ *
+ * `vectors` is a flat `Float32Array` of length `n * dim`; `ids` (if
+ * non-empty) gives external u64 ids in the same order. `k` is capped at
+ * `n` and at 4096 to bound the worst case in a constrained edge runtime.
+ * Returns hits sorted ascending by score.
+ *
+ * **Recall**: 1.0. This is exact, not approximate. Use when n is small
+ * enough that the wall-time fits your budget — at D=128, ~50k vectors is
+ * under 10 ms in browser WASM on a modern laptop.
+ */
+export function searchBruteForceL2(vectors: Float32Array, ids: Float64Array, dim: number, query: Float32Array, k: number): any;
+
+/**
+ * Verify a `table.rulake.json` bundle.
+ *
+ * Parses the JSON (with DoS caps applied), recomputes the SHAKE-256(32)
+ * witness, returns a structured comparison. Returns `Err` (rejected as
+ * a JS exception) on any of: oversize input, missing fields, future
+ * `format_version`, malformed `generation`.
+ */
+export function verifyBundleJson(json: string): any;
